lastVerifiedEvidence.summary: Staging runtime deployed with cutover, prod aliases, and live alerts disabled. ECS desired/running/pending = 1/1/0.
Readiness, admin, site pages, redirect, validation, and S3-first fake seller lead checks passed.

guardrails: Do not merge PRs, push to main, deploy, apply Terraform, change DNS, send live GHL/Slack/email alerts, or mutate production AWS resources without explicit approval.
Do not expose secrets or raw PII in Slack, docs, logs, run-status artifacts, PR evidence, or committed files.
Terraform implementation work was validate/plan-only until Tej approved staging deployment; staging applies are allowed only with cutover/prod aliases/live alerts disabled.
Keep S3-first persistence for seller leads, buyer signups, and deal-interest.
Protect deal exact addresses and hidden/draft media by default.
PR merge and staging deployment are now in scope per Tej 2026-06-06 DoD revision.
Keep enable_dns_cutover=false, enable_prod_alias=false, and enable_live_alerts=false for staging deployment.

Do not change production DNS, attach production CloudFront aliases, mutate legacy production S3/API Gateway/Lambda, or send live AWS/GHL/Slack/email alerts without separate explicit production approval.
nextAction: Done. Await explicit approval before DNS cutover, production alias attachment, live alerts, production Terraform, or production deployment. Recommended hygiene: review/merge follow-up repo changes that capture the staging CloudFront policy fix and evidence.
caveats: staging.gcoffers.com remains legacy until DNS/alias cutover is explicitly approved.
cloudfront.tf no-cache policy fix is local and should be committed/pushed in a follow-up repo hygiene step.
Staging DATABASE_URI uses sslmode=no-verify; revisit CA verification before production cutover.
